trivia_bullets.text: Flavor text: "The ruins of Old Prahv became a wildspace, although all birdsong was silenced by lingering Azorius magic." [Scryfall]. Old Prahv was the original Azorius Senate guildhall — a large white-marble castle destroyed in the climax of Dissension (2006) when the crashed Boros fortress-ship Parhelion exploded at the site. By Return to Ravnica (2012), the ruins had become a wilderness preserve. The flavor text's detail that Azorius residual magic silenced the birds is a small lore horror: the guild's legislative suppression outlasting its own architecture [Scryfall; WebSearch snippet via MTG Wiki Fandom: Prahv].
sources: MTG Wiki Fandom: Prahv via WebSearch snippet

trivia_bullets.text: Urban Burgeoning is a Return to Ravnica (RTR) common, released October 5, 2012. The card is a green aura from the Simic guild, enchanting a land to grant it untap during each other player's untap step — a mana-advantage effect with roots in effects like Seedborn Muse. It depicts the Simic aesthetic of nature colonizing civic architecture literally: the art shows colonnade towers overgrown with trees. RTR was the first Ravnica return set, re-introducing 5 guilds (Izzet, Golgari, Selesnya, Azorius, Rakdos) — Simic arrived in the companion set Gatecrash [Scryfall].

EDHREC rank 13,997 — seldom played in Commander. Its effect is powerful in theory (pseudo-Seedborn Muse on one land) but requires investment in an aura on a land, leaving it vulnerable to two-for-one blowouts. Sees occasional Pauper Commander play as a mana accelerant in green-blue builds [Scryfall legalities].

related_cards.name: Seedborn Muse
related_cards.note: The effect's spiritual predecessor: untap all permanents on each other player's untap step

related_cards.name: Utopia Sprawl
related_cards.note: Simic land-aura in the original Ravnica block; mana-fixation cousin to Urban Burgeoning
